reviews.title: Comfortable, worth the value
reviews.text: The band that originally came with the Fitbit was so uncomfortable. It dig into my wrist and left marks. I ordered another band that was about the same price as all four of these. It was useless. Barely fit, and it wouldn’t keep its position, so I decided to try these. First, I couldn’t believe that the same price got me FOUR bands in different colors! The bands are easily adjustable. Once fitted, these stay in place on the wrist. They’re comfy, and they stretch enough so that I can pull off the Fitbit to charge without having to readjust the band. Though inexpensive, this particular purchase was one of the best I’ve had this year, LOL.

reviews.title: Really love them but 2 already broke after 2 weeks
reviews.text: I really love these, I hate the bands that come with the fitbits. These are really comfortable but 1 band wouldn't work for some reason then another band broke after 2 weeks without even consistently wearing it.

reviews.title: Did NOT Work with Sense 2
reviews.text: Not sure what the issue is, but I could not get any of these bands to engage and lock with the Sense 2.  The tab fits into the slot just fine, but it won't engage the locking tab to hold the band into place.  Pulling on the band at all causes it to simply fall out of the watch case.  The bands themselves look fine, but not locking into place obviously makes them worthless for their intended purpose.

reviews.title: Ok
reviews.text: These performed ok.  It's a good elastic band

reviews.title: Very comfortable
reviews.text: Fits well and allows my arm to breath. I got a rash from the plastic ones

reviews.title: does what it's supposed to
reviews.text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price.
reviews.text: After buying the earlier brands of Fitbit which I loved. I bought myself a Luxe on sale for $100 last December 2021. It didn’t record anything accurately and just didn’t function the way it should. CS was if no help. I switched to Amazfit!  This is a great watch, the battery has lasted over a week. It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

reviews.title: Terrible smart watch, inaccurate and waste of time/money
reviews.text: After 5 months of use I’m done with this. It seems to be getting less accurate every workout. It can’t even get step counts correct. The accuracy of the heart beat monitoring varies too. The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

reviews.title: Good budget watch if you don't need always-on display
reviews.text: Overall, this is a very reasonably priced watch with a lot of functionality. However, the fact that it doesn't have the always-on screen option was a deal breaker for me and I had to replace it with one of their other models.

reviews.title: Did not like this nearly as much as my original amazfit bip watch
reviews.text:
I bought an Amazfit Bip watch a year or more ago and have really been enjoying it The app tracks all my cycling trips and the sleep monitoring is useful. Iam really a big fan of the long battery life of the older model.

This watch has a 9 day battery life which is not nearly as long as the 30 days of the original watch.

This watch meausres blood oxygen levels but taking the measurement requires holding your wrist it the right position and takes much longer that the finger tip device Ihave.

I did not exhaustively evaluate the new app that came with this phone but it did not look nearly as good as the original app.

title: Apple Watch Series 8 [GPS + Cellular 41mm] Smart Watch w/Graphite Stainless Steel Case w/Midnight Sport Band-S/M. Fitness Tracker, Blood Oxygen & ECG Apps, Always-On Retina Display, Water Resistant
features: WHY APPLE WATCH SERIES 8 — Your essential companion for a healthy life is now even more powerful. Advanced sensors provide insights to help you better understand your health. New safety features can get you help when you need it. The bright, Always-On Retina display is easy to read, even when your wrist is down.

CELLULAR CONNECTIVITY — Send a text, make a call, and stream music without your iPhone, even while traveling internationally. Use Family Setup to manage Apple Watch for family members who don’t have an iPhone.
EASILY CUSTOMIZABLE — Available in a range of sizes and materials, with dozens of bands to choose from and watch faces with complications tailored to whatever you’re into.

INNOVATIVE SAFETY FEATURES — Crash Detection and Fall Detection can automatically connect you with emergency services in the event of a severe car crash or a hard fall. And Emergency SOS provides urgent assistance with the press of a button.

ADVANCED HEALTH FEATURES — Temperature sensing is a breakthrough feature that provides deep insights into women’s health. Keep an eye on your blood oxygen. Take an ECG anytime. Get notifications if you have an irregular rhythm. And see how much time you spent in REM, Core, or Deep sleep with Sleep Stages.

SIMPLY COMPATIBLE — It works seamlessly with your Apple devices and services. Unlock your Mac automatically. Find your devices with a tap. Pay and send money with Apple Pay. Apple Watch requires an iPhone 8 or later with the latest iOS version.
INCREDIBLE DURABILITY — Tougher than tough. It’s crack resistant, IP6X-certified dust resistant, and swimproof with WR50 water resistance.

A POWERFUL FITNESS PARTNER — The enhanced Workout app gives you new ways to train, and advanced metrics for more information about your workout performance. And Apple Watch comes with three months of Apple Fitness+ free.
APPS ON YOUR WRIST — With access to tens of thousands of apps on the App Store right on your wrist, Apple Watch is the perfect personal device for whatever you love to do.
LEGAL DISCLAIMERS — This is a summary of the main product features. See below to learn more.

reviews.title: Works - but prefer Samsung products
reviews.text: Long story short - I won an i phone 14 Plus.. I have been a Samsung owner for years including a galaxy watch. But because of the Samsung watch and the fact that if I left my phone at home or at the office I still was able to make and take calls, texts, etc - I purchased this Apple watch to go with  my new free phone (which has cost me a lot $$$ in accessories,  (chargers - what the hell Apple??) but to review the Apple Watch it functions - but I must say for the money it is lacking a lot based on the Galaxy watch.

You get 3 full bands, One black... one gray.. and one light aqua blue - it's more of a very light gray blue.  They are a rubbery ? silicone like? material. Smooth, no sharp edges.  They attached easily. So far, so good.  Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

reviews.title: I was worried that this cable damaged my watch.
reviews.text: I bought this item as extra charging cables for my Vivomove Style, which has physical watch hands in addition to the electronic display. Every time I use this charger instead of the OEM charger, the time shown by the physical hands is hours off and I have to realign.  It also seems to become much less sensitive to touch until I plug it back into the OEM charger to reset it.  The Vivomove Style will keep the time on the physical hands 2 weeks after the battery dies (and I've never seen it not keep accurate time), so it wasn't a dead battery issue.  I was hoping to have more than one cable to charge my watch but based on the issues I'm having,  I'm going back to the OEM cable.

reviews.title: She Loves It!
reviews.text: Picture and video is clear and the games and other things on it are fun. This will also help children to learn time and definitely a good beginners watch for a kid. She figured out how to use many of the features without my help. Lots of great features. She likes to set her alarm clock every morning. This is a great first watch and a perfect gift.

reviews.title: It was ok.
reviews.text:
It's very cute. But let's talk about it.
The Camara was a bit blurry.
Never tried the music option so I don't know.
The pedometer would mark a step even when just moving hands so that wasn't accurate.
We had some trouble setting the alarm clock and set chores.
And the games were cute and fun but killed the battery fast.
I guess I was hopping too much out of this.
It was a gift for my daughter since her timex watch went out. But unfortunately it doesn't have a connect to phone feature where she could receive messages when I'm out and about running errands or late to pick her up from school for that matter. The battery didn't last long and even though my girl liked it, it wasn't too functional for what we needed it for. As I was going to return it my dog had other plans. So yeah the watch didn't survived it's attacker. So it's not dog proofed. Lol 😂

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: Great activity tracker with AMAZING Battery Life.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:e0fda475d57018948d4bdf5116c091bf]] I had a different version activity watch that was way more expensive to match my phone. For the cost this watch does just about everything I need it to for way less money.

A few things I love about the watch...the Battery Life. OMG the battery life! It lasted me a full week with wearing it to bed most nights, through multiple exercise walks and a few work outs, and hundreds of notifications from my messaging apps and phone calls. It's easy to clear the notifications I receive on the watch. I love that it tracks my sleep, heart rate, and stress levels easily. I also tested out in my pool for swimming and it was waterproof without having to shake the watch or turn on any special modes to keep it watertight.

I haven't figured out of it auto detects a work out but that could be because I obviously don't work out that much.

I tested it against my health app on my phone and they were pretty accurate against each other info steps and mileage. I can't figure out how to get the watch and the app to register my GPS coordinates to see the map of my walks but I could be missing the settings.

The app that gives the data is easy to navigate and update any settings. Seems very similar to other track laps I've used.
A few things I'm not liking about this watch...
I feel like the band and the watch itself that comes with the watch is a little too big for my wrist. And I have a bigger wrist for a female at my size. The watch face is bigger than some of my friends apple watches and taller than my old Samsung gear fit watch. I can SEE notifications on the watch but I can't respond or give a quick response to any messages. For Whatsapp or Facebook Messenger I wouldn't expect that but for SMS messages I would like to be able to give a quick text back either by speaking a response or by selecting from some quick options.

Those are really my only 2 complaints. I like this watch and will continue to use!

reviews.title: Such a great Apple Watch dupe!
reviews.text: This smart watch works great! It’s also so cute and the band has lots of holes for adjusting. The only difference I see that’s noticeable is the watch band is a flimsier rubber. But it also makes it feel more comfortable than my Apple Watch. Would make a great gift as well!

reviews.title: Did the job for a week, then it drowned
reviews.text: I charged the watch out of the box.  Followed instructions and setup was pretty much straightforward.  There is a decent amount of sports mode available.  I mainly use run, walk, and swimming.  For walk and run the data captured was okay for duration, calories, distance, number of steps, pace, heart rate. and nighttime monitoring sleep was interesting.  Then I took it for laps in the pool, and that is when it did not do well.  The ad shows person swimming in pool, but I don't believe it can survive in the pool.  because the watch died.

reviews.title: A sluggish, inaccurate, subpar sports/health smart watch.
reviews.text:
I needed a backup sports smart watch for my Apple Watch. Let's check out this Veryfit watch.

What works:
Charged up in about 2-3 hours from 60% battery level.
Easy to install the Veryfit app and pair with my old iPhone 12 Pro.
Has many of the basic sports watch functions: stopwatch, alarm, timer, along with biometrics: Heart rate, Blood oxygen monitoring, Stress level, Breath training, Sleep, etc.
Big, easy to read watch face, only a bit narrower than my 44mm Apple Watch.
A variety of watch faces to download.
Nice, soft, comfortable watch straps.

What don't work:
Once linked up to my iPhone, app takes some effort to set up.
Can only receive notifications and phone call alerts, no relay of calls.
Heart rate monitor is erratic with a wide range of random readings. My resting heart rate is 56bpm yet this watch measures a range of 62 to 125bpm at various times.
Requires about 25s to get each biometric reading.
What's up with the 3-2-1 countdown to start each exercise? Start/Stop is what most users prefer.
Perhaps the Android version works better?
Don't expect too much from this $34.99 smart watch (after -30% off coupon on Feb. 19, 2023) but there are other smart watches in this price range that function better.

reviews.title: I like it
reviews.text: It's a little bulky but not enough for me not to still wear it every day

reviews.title: 5-star product!
reviews.text: You have to love it when a product, even something as simple as a thin piece of plastic, outperforms your expectations. Packaged extremely well with everything you'd possibly need, I had my watch cleaned, prepped and completed in minutes. I will use the Skinomi brand for my iPad, Kindle Fire and Samsung Galaxy S III as soon as their old (non-Skinomi) screen protectors need replacement.

reviews.title: Really impressed
reviews.text: Got this for my Sony LiveView and while I was shocked to see that the installation process was more than other screen protectors (ie cell phone screen protectors), installation was pretty simple. After it dries, you can't even tell a screen protector is on!

reviews.title: It's soft
reviews.text:
Like another reviewer had mentioned, they are soft. I used a rubber squeegee that I've used on countless other screen savers, and it actually damaged the skin. It has permanent "bubbles" (more like dents) in it that will not go away. Other than that, the fit is perfect. And the skin comes very nicely packaged with everything you could ever need, including a bottle of soapy water for the application.

The attention to detail by Skinomi is second to none. The company gets five stars, but I've got to deduct one for the soft material. Skinomi did include a squeegee, but it's too big for use with this skin. Had I cut it down to size and used it, I may not have "dents" now.

reviews.title: Huge disappointment
reviews.text:
I am a big supporter of Polar products, my M400 was an absolute beast. I've since moved on to the Vantage V and it's by far the best training watch I've ever owned. This watch was a mess from the start...

I can't in good faith give this anything but 1 star, which it earned because of the HR monitor built in and I didn't seem to have any issues with tracking or accuracy, but that is pretty common faire these days and warrant just 1 star. I was just so damn tired of the HR strap that this was great at the time.

The power use or battery life (could never figure it out) was complete garbage. I understand that the target demo for this watch was never the ultra-marathon crowd, but this thing could barely make it through a day let alone a workout within that day. I disabled the "smart watch" features, enabled the smart watch features, ran the battery dead between wears, tried to always keep it charged, etc., And nothing seemed to matter. The battery would go from full to "not enough charge for workout" so erratically that it felt like keeping this thing working was the real workout. Mid-run, EVEN AFTER DISCONNECTING FROM THE PHONE, it would drain so quickly that it was always a gamble if it would make it through a 3 hr run (tops). Also, when this thing starts dying, you lose your tracking data first, then the HR, then it's a brick on your wrist. All within about 14-15% to zero, which was also pretty erratic. The sleep monitoring features are a wash because you have to charge this thing constantly so it only makes sense to do it at night.

The 2 button layout was a failure... Swiping is great (in theory) but the screen is pretty small, the gestures awkward, "swipe to nothing" screens and such made navigation miserable. (Possibly an Android Wear issue but no one needs to change their watchface swiping in 2-3 directions.) Secondly, this is a workout watch, you get sweaty (or drenched here in FL). This renders the watch's screen completely useless and I can't tell you how many corners I stood at pressing the screen trying to resume a run, change the metrics on my screen, etc. The standard workout screen also requires you swipe down for some stats so you just give up after a while. For normal daily use, I kept changing watch faces (which were very clunky to modify) or ending up at the settings menu. An Android Wear update resolved some of this but very iffy at best.

Once said run was done, syncing became an issue. I finally figured out that if you completely disconnected the watch, turned off any Bluetooth on the phone, did the workout, then slowly added the connections back, you had the best chance of the watch properly syncing with Flow and Strava. 60-70% of the time... If you forgot, all bets were off. I don't generally run with the phone and watch, but the few times I did it destroyed the battery life because it kept pinging the watch (also may be Android Wear), even when the link was severed/watch disconnected.

Onto the smart watch features. I won't beat a dead horse with the things noted above, but other than vibrating for calls or text messages, this was mostly useless. The slow response and connection time between the watch and my Pixel 2 had me constantly talking and repeating myself to my wrist like a lunatic. Then there are the 2-3 confirmations and need for a notary to send anything from the watch.... The mic idea is a good one and I suppose having the OK Google feature might be handy, but it mostly steers you back to your phone for anything useful, so I just ended up picking up the phone and bypassing the watch 90% of the time. This of course is not to mention the drain on the battery... I was really hopeful about the wrist nav and Google maps since I travel a bit but it just didn't pan out and I continued to rely on just the phone. They also sell that as a running feature (navigation) but you've got to pair with the phone so we're back to battery concerns.

The watch itself sits in a rubberized strap for removal/cleaning, which makes it large/bulky. Was difficult wearing under a dress shirt as a daily activity monitor. The Vantage V isn't small either, but it has a more classic watch look rather than time-traveler band so it blends better and I can wear under a cuff. If this watch isn't separated and rinsed after each use, it gets funky fast. Lotta nooks and crannies in there, be sure to clean it well or smell like a locker room. The band also doesn't breath at all so I would have to take days off wearing it because a rash would form. Wearing it loosely to help with this was uncomfortable due to its size so there wasn't really a win to be found there.

Polar really missed the mark on this one and I was really bummed that this was the direction they were taking their product line. Thankfully, right at the apex of frustrations, the Vantage V came out and they redeemed themselves. It's not a small price jump and I don't know too much about the Vantage M, but literally anything would be better than this watch and the V is by far the most impressive item they've delivered on.

reviews.title: Don't be dumb
reviews.text: Buy it

reviews.title: Ok
reviews.text: Ok

reviews.title: Not Rating Watch
reviews.text: I’ve spent thousands of dollars on Polar watches, speedometers, etc, for my bikes and gym. I’ve had trouble off and on with charging cables. Although they have been great to replace charging cables, they outmode their products, so they become useless even tho they still work. I had a bad accident so was rehabbing for two years and had just started training in my gym and on bikes to compete again. I just got a notice in the mail, that the online training program will no longer be available I would have to buy a Polar Beat. I still need to know V2o, heart rate zones, training zones, so many other things that helped me train to my full potential. Even my Polar gym watches don’t work. I guess I can throw them out, although they still work. That being said, they make a good product, but so many become outmoded so you have to keep buying them.

reviews.title: So much potential but not worth the headache
reviews.text: I have had this for almost one year.  Very soon after I got it, it crashed.  I called customer service and reset it.  It took awhile and I was annoyed but the problem resolved.  Then again, similar issue.  This time the bluetooth and GPS stopped working.  I have used it for months with very limited capability - to track steps and heart rate - but decided to try to do another factory reset.  Lo and behold, that did not work so I guess I will be returning it to Polar.  I bought this because the heart rate monitor was supposed to be the best, and it is very accurate.  But it seems that Polar is not able to consistently deliver a quality product.

title: iTECH Fusion 2 S Smartwatch Fitness Tracker, Heart Rate, Step Counter, Sleep Monitor, Message, IP67 Water Resistant for Men and Women, Touch Screen, Compatible with iPhone and Android (Black Mesh)
description: Looking for a smartwatch that won't break the bank? The iTech Fusion 2 S smartwatch includes high tech features at an affordable price. The iTech Fusion 2 S watch doubles as a fitness tracker - including a calorie counter, pedometer, and a sleep monitor! Receive phone notifications, weather forecasts, and control music right from your wrist! Also includes sports mode such as cycling, baseball, basketball, football, tennis, soccer, golf and walking. So many great features on a full touchscreen dial! Keep up with your busy lifestyle with the iTech Fusion 2 S smartwatch. Complete with convenience, this watch features a motion gesture, so you'll be able to view the screen just by turning your wrist.
features: The iTech Fusion 2 S smartwatch keeps you motivated and on track with your fitness and weightloss goals. Get alerts from the Sedentary Reminder for when it is time to start a new activity, and feel rewarded when you reach your workout goals with the Achievement Notifications!
Monitor the quality of your workouts and sleep patterns with the Calorie Tracker and Sleep Monitor. Assures that you are getting the proper rest to achieve optimum exercise quality.
Advanced Multi-Sports Mode technology records data from your favorite activities, including running, cycling, hiking, basketball, tennis and football.
Pair your iTech Fusion 2 S smart watch with the iTech Wearables app - compatible with most iOS and Android smartphones.
Certified IP67 - Water Resistant technology keeps your watch undamaged rain or shine! Watch is durable for up to 3 feet of water.

reviews.title: Amazing Feature Packed Smart Watch at a Spectacular Cost
reviews.text:
This VALYV K22 Military Sport Watch is packed with an amazing number of smart features and provided seamless integration with my Samsung smart phone. The display is crystal-clear, and the audio capability and volume are very impressive. In general, it’s a very handsome watch and has a very intuitive user interface. The price is unbelievably low in comparison to some of the other smart watches on the market.
I especially enjoy the built-in fitness, activity, and sports tracking features which have already proven to be invaluable while training at the gym as well as general health monitoring during my activity throughout the day.
This watch charges quickly using the included USB magnetically connected cable and the battery is long lasting once charged. The user interface is customizable through the app – I preferred the default one that it shipped with… but there are MANY other options to choose from.
I summary, after spending many years not even wearing a watch… I am very happy with this purchase and can definitely recommend the purchase of this useful product to others.

reviews.title: Really great for the price!
reviews.text: I don’t spend a lot of money on things like this so I didn’t know what to expect since it’s not $300+… it’s not bad for the price! It works well, the stats seem fairly accurate, except the blood pressure which is always showing ridiculously good (I wish)!! I have high blood pressure and this watch has me at 117/ 68 most of the time. Other then that the only problem I have is the charge! It has a cable with 2 tiny magnets, you have to line them up just so and as soon as you’ve got it, it pops off!!

reviews.title: works to add security to fitbit watch band
reviews.text:
Good. I have been using these bands as extra security on my fitbit bands since I first got a fitbit dating back to 2015. I have had several styles since then, and I always make sure to have a pack of these security bands on hand. First it was because the fitbit band was just a pressure close band that easily popped off, then it was a matter of keeping a regular watch band style extra secure. These work on the regular width bands.

When I was ready to buy more of these security bands, I was about to buy some that are made for fitbit, but then I realized that those have a protrusion for locking into the band. I really just wanted something to slip on with no need to make use of the band holes to secure it. These are exactly what I wanted. Nice variety of colors.

reviews.title: Never loose a magic band or watch again
reviews.text: After loosing a magic band or two at Disney I thought about how my watches have a second clasp. This product fills that void. It can slip over the band and help keep it secure on your wrist even after taking sweaters on or off, rummaging in bags or pockets. I'm confident this could also replace a missing watch band if need be.
